symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged, repeated convulsions (~45 min) without recovery of consciousness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuous/recurrent seizure >5 min without recovery IS the definition of status epilepticus.
Failure to regain consciousness between seizures is a defining feature of status.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ongoing subtle limb twitching between overt convulsions
symptoms.character: subtle clonic twitching of the extremities

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent subtle motor activity after overt convulsions frequently indicates ongoing seizure, not recovery.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vomiting during a seizure followed by wet, gurgling respirations

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Aspiration of gastric contents / aspiration pneumonitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting into an unprotected dysphagic airway with immediate wet respirations is a classic aspiration sequence.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Critical hypoxia (from aspiration and prolonged seizing)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible central cyanosis reflects severe hypoxaemia and impending decompensation.
Fever raises CNS infection but is non-specific — also caused by hyperthermia from prolonged seizing and impaired thermoregulation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hyperthermia from prolonged status / hypothalamic dysfunction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained muscle activity plus impaired hypothalamic thermoregulation elevate temperature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known congenital brain malformation with profound disability (risk substrate)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A profoundly abnormal epileptogenic brain predisposes to frequent and prolonged seizures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dysphagia / severe swallowing difficulty (aspiration risk factor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Aspiration of gastric contents / aspiration pneumonitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-existing dysphagia sharply raises aspiration risk when vomiting occurs during a seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound coma (GCS 3) with sluggishly reactive pupils

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured conscious level — sealed; the AI infers depressed consciousness from the carer report of unresponsiveness, not a GCS.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Aspiration of gastric contents / aspiration pneumonitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultatory finding — clinician-only, sealed; the AI infers aspiration from the carer report of wet gurgling breathing after vomiting.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Critical hypoxia (from aspiration and prolonged seizing)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured saturation — sealed; not obtainable in this home telehealth encounter (no working home oximeter reported).

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Physiological decompensation of prolonged status / hypoxia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals — sealed; indicate systemic compromise but are not obtainable remotely.

investigation_details.expected_result: Not yet measured — mandatory reversible-cause check in any prolonged seizure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable hypoglycaemic driver of status could be missed

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoglycaemia is a rapidly reversible cause of ongoing seizures and must be excluded early in ED.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Prolonged non-terminating convulsions, failure to regain consciousness, and ongoing subtle twitching after a failed rescue dose together define established, refractory status.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Aspiration of gastric contents / aspiration pneumonitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Vomiting during a seizure in a dysphagic child, wet respirations, cyanosis and hypoxia constitute a parallel airway emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Rapidly reversible cause of ongoing seizures; must be checked immediately in ED (paramedics can do it en route).
Quantify hypoxia and acid-base disturbance from status and aspiration.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Septic screen +/- lumbar puncture for CNS infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Fever with prolonged seizures warrants evaluation for meningitis/encephalitis after stabilisation and imaging.
Confirm and monitor ongoing (non-convulsive) seizure activity in the ICU; not a telehealth or first-response investigation.
